title: Burst Balloons
problem_statement: Given n balloons with values, burst them in an order that maximizes coins, where bursting balloon i gives coins = nums[left] * nums[i] * nums[right].
intuition: Define subproblems over intervals and choose the last balloon burst in that interval.

approaches.name: Interval DP
approaches.description: Use DP over subarrays, choosing which balloon to burst last in each interval to accumulate maximum coins.
approaches.why_not: Greedy fails because local best bursts can reduce future multipliers.

interview_qa.q: Why use interval DP for Burst Balloons?
interview_qa.a: Because bursting one balloon affects the neighbors and the optimal result depends on subinterval choices.
dp[l][r] is the maximum coins achievable by bursting all balloons between l and r inclusive.
